tunnel-entrance.name: Tunnel Entrance
tunnel-entrance.description: You climb down into a wide maintenance tunnel. Faded {pink:radiation warnings} line the walls. The air is warmer here, thick with dust that coats your tongue. Pipes overhead hiss softly — the only sound besides your own breathing.

examine.warnings: {pink:CAUTION — ELEVATED RAD LEVELS BEYOND THIS POINT}. The paint is cracked and peeling, but the message is clear enough.
examine.pipes: Coolant, water, waste — all dead lines now. Empty tubes gathering dust for four decades.

junction.name: Tunnel Junction
junction.description:
A four-way intersection. Water drips from a ceiling crack in a steady, hollow rhythm. Signs on the walls point each direction:

{dim:NORTH} — Cryo Level Access
{dim:WEST} — Generator Room {pink:(⚠ RAD)}
{dim:EAST} — Server Room B
{dim:SOUTH} — Surface Access

The {pink:radiation} grows stronger to the west.

examine.signs: Standard facility wayfinding. ATLAS Infrastructure Corp branding — clean, official, and 40 years out of date.

water.text:
Groundwater seeping through a hairline crack. Slightly {green:luminescent}. Definitely irradiated. Don't drink it.

As you crouch to look, a {purple:memory fragment} surfaces unbidden — triggered by the posture, the smell of damp concrete, something your body remembers.

examine.ceiling: Reinforced concrete. 40 years underground and still holding. They built this place to outlast the people inside it.

generator-room.name: Generator Room

examine.generator: ATLAS GEN-IV Fusion Cell. Massive. It kept this facility breathing for decades, and the spent fuel is still cooking the air around it.

panel.hackText: You push deeper into the system. Buried in the maintenance logs — a {purple:memory fragment}, recorded by your neural implant before you went under.

choices.text: Grab the power cell from the rack
Get out before the RAD gets worse

server-room.name: Server Room B
server-room.description:
Rows of dead server racks — dark slabs of silicon and steel. But one rack near the entrance still breathes, its status LEDs blinking {green:green}.

A {cyan:terminal} is bolted to the active rack. The air hums with residual charge, tingling against your skin.

encounter.introText: As you reach for the terminal, a {pink:maintenance drone} detaches from the ceiling. Its red eye locks onto you. Corrupted firmware has turned routine protocols into something predatory.

terminal.hackText: Deeper in the data stream, a cached {purple:memory fragment} surfaces like a body from dark water.

examine.racks: Dead servers. The {amber:circuit boards} and {amber:wiring} inside are still worth salvaging.
examine.drone: Standard ATLAS maintenance drone — or it was. 40 years of firmware rot turned routine maintenance into territorial aggression.

locked-door.name: Locked Security Door
locked-door.description:
A reinforced security door blocks the tunnel. A {pink:red light} pulses on the lock mechanism. An {cyan:access panel} is mounted beside it.

Beyond the steel, you can feel the signal pulling. The surface is close.

conditionalDesc.text: The reinforced security door stands open, lock mechanism {green:bypassed}. The tunnel slopes south toward a faint glow — {bright:daylight}.

south.failText: {pink:The security door is locked.} You need to bypass the {cyan:access panel}.

s.failText: {pink:The security door is locked.} You need to bypass the {cyan:access panel}.

examine.door: Heavy steel. Military grade. Built to keep people out — or in.

panel.text: A keycard reader and digital access panel. The keycard slot takes standard ATLAS facility cards. The digital panel might be {cyan:hackable} with the right skills.
panel.hackText:
The encryption is ancient — 40-year-old protocols. Your {purple:implant} makes short work of it.

{green:ACCESS GRANTED.} The security door grinds open with a hiss.

examine.light: Steady red pulse. The lock system is still powered and functional after all this time.

choices.text: Swipe the Cryo Keycard through the reader
choices.resultText: You swipe the keycard. The reader beeps — {green:ACCESS GRANTED}. The door grinds open.

choices.text: Shoulder the door open by force
choices.resultText: {pink:The door doesn't budge.} Military-grade steel. You'd need explosives or a bypass — not brute force.

tunnel-exit.name: Tunnel Exit
tunnel-exit.description:
The tunnel slopes upward toward a rectangle of pale light. Real light. {bright:Daylight}.

The air shifts — dusty but fresh, carrying scents of earth and slow decay. After 40 years underground, the surface is steps away.

The signal in your {purple:implant} beats steady and clear, like a second pulse.

examine.light: Natural light from above. Weak, filtered through dust — but real. It's been 40 years since you last saw it, even if you don't remember.
examine.signal: The signal pulses through your {purple:implant} like a heartbeat. Steady. Directional. It's calling you west.

choices.resultText: {dim:You sit against the tunnel wall and catch your breath. The pale light from above is strangely comforting.}
